Home › The Radiation-Dominated Era

The Radiation-Dominated Era

The Radiation-Dominated Era was the first major epoch of the 4D Block Universe, extending from the end of 4D Inflation to approximately 50,000 years after the Big Bang. During this period, the 4D Energy Density of the 4D Subatomic Vacuum was primarily composed of relativistic particles (photons and neutrinos), whose 4D Total Dynamics dictated a rapid expansion rate.

1. Scaling of Radiation Density

Because radiation possesses 4D Pressure (\( P = ho c^2 / 3 \)), its density dilutes faster than matter as space expands. While matter density falls as the volume (\( a^{-3} \)), radiation density falls as \( a^{-4} \) because the 4D Cosmological Redshift also reduces the energy of each individual photon. This 4D Logical Basis explains why radiation, despite being dominant at the start, eventually gave way to 4D Matter Domination.

2. Expansion Dynamics (\( a \propto t^{1/2} \))

In the radiation era, the 4D Friedmann Equations yield a scale factor expansion of \( a(t) \propto \sqrt{t} \). this is fastest standard (non-inflationary) 4D Expansion Rate. The high temperature and density of this epoch allowed for Big Bang Nucleosynthesis, where the first 4D Ontology of light elements (Hydrogen and Helium) was forged in the primordial 4D Subatomic Vacuum.

3. Transition to Matter

As the universe cooled, the 4D Energy of radiation dropped below the rest-mass energy of matter. This point of Equality marks the end of the radiation era. This transition was critical for Ontological Emergence, as the slower expansion rate of the 4D Matter Era allowed gravity to finally overcome the expansion and begin the 4D Causal Topology of galaxy formation.

Key Theoretical Identities

Interact with identities to explore their semantic depth.

  • Driving Force [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ ho_r \propto a^{-4} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Driving Force: The high pressure of light that prevented matter from clumping in the early universe.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Energy Loss [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ a(t) \propto t^{1/2} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Energy Loss: The dual mechanism (volume increase + wavelength stretch) that caused radiation density to plummet.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Technical Relation [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ H(t) = \frac{1}{2t} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Mathematical Context: This formula describes a key relationship within The Radiation-Dominated Era.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.
